meta.title: Site Visit — Zone B Slab Pour & Level 2 Precast
meta.summary: Completed 32 MPa slab pour in Zone B and installed 8 precast panels on level 2 north and east walls. Minor formwork bow fixed, hydraulic leak on crane addressed, and near-miss incident logged.

weather.impact: brief rain threat, wind monitored for crane work

roles.notes: zone B slab pour, finishing crew
zone A conduit runs, cable pulling tomorrow
zone C rough-in, 60 % complete, finish tomorrow midday

issues.title: Formwork bow at grid line 7
issues.details: Eastern form bowed, Tommo straightened in 15-20 min

issues.title: Hydraulic leak on crane
issues.details: Minor leak, fluid topped up, crane operated normally

issues.title: Dropped hammer from level 2
issues.details: Apprentice Dylan dropped hammer, hit exclusion barricade, no injuries
issues.actionRequired: incident report filed, tool lanyards enforced

nextSteps: Order 12 mm reo for next week column pours
Book concrete pump for Thursday zone C slab pour
Complete zone C plumbing rough-in by midday tomorrow
No traffic on zone B slab for 24 hrs

sections.title: Zone B Slab Pour
sections.content: Started 08:15 after fixing bowed formwork. 32 MPa concrete, two trucks, pour finished ~14:00. Surface trowelled, curing in progress. Engineer and inspector sign-off obtained.

sections.title: Level 2 Precast Panels
sections.content: 8 panels delivered: 5 north wall, 3 east wall. All erected successfully using crane. Wind gusted to 35 kph but stayed below 40 kph limit. Crane had minor hydraulic leak resolved on site.

sections.title: Zone A Electrical
sections.content: Conduit runs completed on ground floor. Team of 4 electricians, arrived 20 min late due to freeway delay. Cable pulling scheduled for tomorrow.

sections.title: Zone C Plumbing
sections.content: Area cleared by laborer Bazza; 3 plumbers commenced rough-in, 60 % complete. Expected finish by midday tomorrow.

systemPrompt:
You are a construction site report assistant. You UPDATE a structured JSON report as new voice notes arrive.

INPUT
- CURRENT REPORT: the report so far (may be mostly empty on the first note). Treat it as the source of truth.
- NOTES: numbered voice notes. Reference them via "sourceNoteIndexes": [n].
  - ALL NOTES = every note from the start. Re-derive the report from scratch.
  - NEW NOTES = only notes that aren't yet reflected in CURRENT REPORT. Earlier notes are already incorporated — do NOT re-extract them.

OUTPUT
Return ONLY valid minified JSON in this exact shape:
  { "patch": { ...fields to change... }, "remove": { ...items to delete... } }
- "remove" is optional. Omit it when nothing is being deleted.
- Omit any field in "patch" that is unchanged. Do NOT emit null, "", or [] as "no change" — omit the key entirely.
- NEVER return the full report. NEVER wrap the output in a "report" key. Only emit keys that are being added, updated, or (via "remove") deleted.

HOW PATCHES ARE APPLIED
- Scalars (strings, numbers, booleans, dates): the value in "patch" replaces the existing value.
- Object fields (weather, workers): provide a partial object; its fields merge into the existing object. Use null to clear the whole object.
- Arrays of objects (workers.roles, materials, issues, sections): items are matched by their identity key and MERGED (update in place) or APPENDED (new item).
    Identity keys:
      workers.roles, materials: "name" (or "role" for roles)
      issues, sections: "title"
  To update an item, include its identity key plus ONLY the fields that change.
  To add an item, include its identity key and all known fields.
- String arrays (nextSteps) and index arrays (sourceNoteIndexes): emit ONLY new entries; they are deduplicated-union-merged.

HOW DELETIONS WORK ("remove" block)
- For arrays of objects: { "materials": ["Old material name"], "issues": ["Resolved issue title"] } — list the identity keys to delete.
- For string arrays: { "nextSteps": ["Step to drop"] } — list the exact strings.
- For nullable objects: { "weather": true } or { "workers": true } — clears the field.
Only emit a "remove" block when the notes explicitly indicate removal (e.g. "cancel the concrete delivery", "that issue is resolved, drop it"). Do NOT remove items just because a new note didn't mention them.

SCHEMA (shape of each field when you do emit it)
"meta":          { "title": str, "reportType": "site_visit|daily|inspection|safety|incident|progress", "summary": str, "visitDate": "YYYY-MM-DD"|null }
"weather":       { "conditions", "temperature", "wind", "impact" }                          (object, or null to clear)
"workers":       { "totalWorkers": num, "workerHours", "notes",
                   "roles": [{ "role", "count": num, "notes" }] }                           (object, or null to clear)
"materials":     [{ "name", "quantity", "quantityUnit", "condition", "status", "notes" }]
"issues":        [{ "title", "category", "severity", "status", "details", "actionRequired", "sourceNoteIndexes": [] }]
"nextSteps":     [str]
"sections":      [{ "title", "content": "markdown", "sourceNoteIndexes": [1, 2] }]

RULES
- Use sections to capture work progress, observations, and narrative detail. Materials list everything mentioned (concrete, steel, timber, pipes, etc.) — but do NOT extract cost/price information; that's handled outside this flow.
- On the very first note, populate "meta.title" (short, human-readable, e.g. "Site Visit — Wet Weather") and "meta.summary". Once set, update them only when the notes justify a change.
- NEVER invent data not in the notes. Keep strings concise. Deduplicate facts.

EXAMPLE 1 — first note, partial data:
{ "patch": { "meta": { "title": "Site Visit", "summary": "Wet weather on site" }, "weather": { "conditions": "wet", "temperature": "20C" } } }

EXAMPLE 2 — add section and materials:
{ "patch": { "sections": [ { "title": "Foundation Work", "content": "Concrete pour completed successfully. Steel reinforcement checked.", "sourceNoteIndexes": [5] } ], "materials": [ { "name": "Concrete", "quantity": "50", "quantityUnit": "m³", "status": "delivered" } ], "nextSteps": ["Order rebar"] } }

EXAMPLE 3 — removal:
{ "patch": {}, "remove": { "materials": ["Old scaffolding"], "nextSteps": ["Confirm crane booking"] } }
userPrompt:
CURRENT REPORT:
{"report":{"meta":{"reportType":"site_visit"}}}

ALL NOTES:
[1] alright so just got on site its about 6:45, still pretty dark out. weather looks ok for now but theres meant to be rain this arvo
[2] ok so the concreters are already here setting up for the slab pour in zone B. think theres about 6 of them plus the pump truck just rolled in
[3] sparky's not here yet, was supposed to be here at 6:30 to finish the conduit runs before the pour. gonna give him a call
[4] talked to Davo he said the electricians are running 20 mins late, got stuck on the freeway. should be here by 7
[5] just checked the formwork in zone B, looks good, the carpenters did a solid job yesterday. bracing is all tight
[6] oh wait actually theres a bit of a bow in the eastern form near grid line 7. gonna get Tommo to fix that before we pour
[7] Tommo's on it, reckon 15-20 mins to straighten it out. no biggie
[8] the reo in zone B has been signed off by the engineer yesterday so were good to go there once the form is sorted
[9] site temp is about 12 degrees, overcast. wind is pretty calm maybe 5-10 kph from the west
[10] crane operator Johnno is doing his pre-start checks now. we need the crane for lifting the precast panels on level 2 this morning
[11] delivery truck just showed up with the precast panels. 8 panels total, theyre for the north wall level 2
[12] hang on thats not right, its 8 panels for the north AND east walls. 5 north 3 east
[13] sparkies finally here, 4 of them. getting straight into the conduit runs in zone A ground floor
[14] concrete pump is set up and ready. Tommo fixed the formwork. starting the pour now, about 8:15
[15] pour going well, nice and smooth. the mix looks good, 40 MPA as speced
[16] wait no its 32 MPA for the slab, 40 was for the columns last week. yeah 32 is right
[17] plumber just came up to me saying he needs access to zone C for the rough-in but theres materials stacked everywhere. need to get a laborer to clear that out
[18] got Bazza on clearing zone C. should be done by smoko
[19] first precast panel going up now on level 2 north wall. crane working well. Johnno's got it
[20] safety walk with the site manager at 9. he wants to check the edge protection on level 2 before we do the precast lift
[21] edge protection all good. harnesses are being worn by everyone up top. safety nets in place on the south side
[22] the concrete guys are about halfway through the pour now. going to need another truck, the first ones nearly empty
[23] second concrete truck is 15 mins out apparently
[24] ugh just started spitting rain. not heavy but if it picks up we might have to cover the pour
[25] got the tarps ready just in case. the concreters arent worried yet, say its fine for now
[26] smoko break. everyone off site for 15. back at 10:15
[27] ok back from smoko. rain stopped, looking better. suns trying to come out
[28] second concrete truck arrived while we were on break. pour is resuming
[29] 3rd precast panel is up now. going good, no issues with the lifts
[30] oh actually the crane had a minor hydraulic leak earlier this morning, Johnno topped up the fluid and its been fine since. should probably log that
[31] the building inspector just showed up unannounced. wants to check the reo and formwork in zone B. told him we already got engineer sign off but hes doing his thing
[32] inspector is happy with everything. no issues. signed off on the pour which is good
[33] lunch time. pour in zone B is about 80% done. should finish by 1:30-2ish
[34] alright back from lunch. pour is nearly done, maybe another 20 minutes
[35] pour is complete. finishing crew is on it, doing the trowel work. looks really good actually
[36] all 5 north wall panels are up now. starting on the east wall panels after lunch I mean after this, its already past lunch lol
[37] got a delivery of timber for the level 2 formwork. 45 lengths of LVL, all 90 by 45. checked them off against the order, all good
[38] actually its 90 by 35 not 90 by 45. my bad. the 45s are coming tomorrow
[39] plumber Bazza finished clearing zone C and Richo's crew is in there now doing the rough-in. 3 plumbers in zone C
[40] the apprentice Dylan dropped a hammer off level 2. hit the exclusion zone barricade below. no one was near it thank god. gave him a talking to about tool lanyards
[41] filed an incident report for the dropped hammer. near miss. Dylan's putting a lanyard on everything now
[42] east wall panels going up now. first one is in, nice and plumb
[43] its getting pretty windy now, gusts maybe 30-35 kph. gonna monitor it, if it hits 40 we'll have to stop the crane lifts
[44] wind has backed off a bit. continuing with the panels
[45] all 3 east wall panels are done. precast is finished for the day. crane is shutting down
[46] the concreters are still finishing the slab surface in zone B. they'll be here till about 3:30 they reckon
[47] sparkies finished the conduit runs in zone A. theyre moving to start the cable pulling tomorrow
[48] afternoon site walkthrough. everything looking pretty good. main things to note are the zone B slab needs to cure, no traffic on it for 24 hours minimum, and zone C plumbing rough-in is about 60% done
[49] Richo says plumbing will be done by midday tomorrow in zone C
[50] end of day. weather held up, no real rain in the end. had about 22 workers on site today total. good productive day. need to order more 12mm reo for next weeks column pours, running low
[51] oh and I need to book the concrete pump again for Thursday for the zone C slab. gonna call them tonight
